# Procurement Management Plan

## Purpose
Define the procurement strategy, contract approach, and governance for vendor engagement.

## Audience
Procurement Lead, Project Manager, Legal, Finance, Vendor Manager.

## Inputs
- RFP template (`docs/templates/finance/rfp-template-cross.md`).
- Vendor evaluation scorecard (`docs/templates/finance/vendor-evaluation-scorecard-cross.xlsx`).
- Contract summary (`docs/templates/finance/contract-summary-template-cross.md`).

## 1. Procurement Objectives
Describe sourcing goals, constraints, and success criteria.

## 2. Roles & Responsibilities
| Role | Responsibilities | Approval Authority |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

## 3. Contract Types & Selection Criteria
List approved contract types (fixed price, T&M, SLA-based) and selection criteria.

## 4. Procurement Timeline
| Milestone | Target Date | Owner | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |

## 5. Solicitation & Evaluation Process
Describe the solicitation steps, evaluation panels, and scoring approach.

## 6. Contract Management Procedures
Define onboarding, reporting, invoice review, and performance management.

## 7. Make-or-Buy Analysis
Summarize criteria and decision rationale.

## 8. Procurement Risks
| Risk | Impact | Mitigation | Owner |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |

## Completion Checklist
- [ ] Contract types and selection criteria approved.
- [ ] Timeline and evaluation process documented.
- [ ] Procurement risks and mitigations identified.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Procurement plan aligns with legal and finance requirements.
- Vendor selection process is transparent and auditable.
